title: Add coupon discount support to charge endpoint
summary: This PR adds coupon-discount support to the charge endpoint but has several blocking defects that prevent it from merging safely. The most critical issue is a PCI/PII data-loss: a raw `console.log` prints the full request body—including card number and CVC—to stdout, completely bypassing the Pino logger's redaction configuration. On top of that, the core feature is silently broken: `payload.amount` is mutated to equal `finalAmount` before the subtraction, so `discount_amount` is always 0. A negative-amount footgun in `FixedCouponStrategy` and a catch-all silent swallow round out the must-fix list. There are also consistent standards violations (snake_case fields in a camelCase API, `console.log` where pino should be used, `any` cast, `var`, `==`) and no tests for any of the new logic.

findings.title: console.log dumps raw card number and CVC to stdout
findings.message: `console.log('DEBUG charge body:', JSON.stringify(theData))` serialises the entire request body and writes it to stdout before Zod validation strips anything. The project already uses `pino` with explicit `redact` paths for `card.number` and `card.cvc` (see `src/logger.ts` lines 9-17) specifically to prevent PCI data from appearing in logs. This line bypasses that protection entirely. Remove the debug log; if you need request-level logging, rely on the `pinoHttp` middleware already wired in `src/app.ts` or call `logger.debug` with a hand-picked, field-safe object.

findings.title: discount_amount is always 0 — payload.amount mutated before subtraction
findings.message: Line 23 writes `payload.amount = finalAmount`, so by the time line 35 evaluates `payload.amount - finalAmount` the two operands are identical and the result is always `0`. The discount is correctly applied to the gateway charge but is never reflected in the response. Fix: capture the original amount before the coupon is applied—e.g. `const originalAmount = payload.amount;`—and use that in the subtraction: `response.discountAmount = originalAmount - finalAmount;`.

findings.title: Silent catch swallows all coupon errors, masking failures
findings.message: The inner `try/catch` with `// TODO` in the body silently discards any exception thrown during coupon processing. If coupon logic ever throws (e.g., due to a future validation guard), the error disappears, the charge proceeds at full price with no indication to the caller, and nothing appears in the logs. Either remove the inner try/catch and let errors propagate normally, or explicitly handle the error (log it via `logger.warn`, return a 400, etc.).

findings.title: FixedCouponStrategy can return a negative amount, causing an unhandled 500
findings.message: `return amount - this.amt` has no floor guard. When the discount exceeds the charge (e.g., TENOFF—1000 cents—applied to a $3.00 charge of 300 cents), `apply()` returns `-700`. That negative number is then passed to `paymentsService.charge()`, which calls `assertPositiveAmount()` (`src/domain/money.ts` line 14-18). `assertPositiveAmount` throws a plain `Error`, not an `AppError`, which the `errorHandler` maps to an opaque 500. The caller gets no actionable error. Fix: add `return Math.max(0, amount - this.amt)` or throw a domain-level `AppError` with a 400 before the charge is attempted.

findings.title: Casting transaction to `any` and mutating it in-place corrupts the stored domain object
findings.message: `const response: any = transaction` is a reference copy, not a clone. Assigning `response.discount_amount` and `response.coupon_code` therefore mutates the same object that was already handed to `transactionRepository.save()`. In `InMemoryTransactionRepository`, `save()` stores the reference directly (line 12 of `transactionRepository.ts`), so the stored transaction now carries response-layer fields. A subsequent `GET /transactions/:id` will return those fields too, leaking presentation concerns into the domain model. Fix: build a separate response DTO—`res.status(201).json({ ...transaction, discountAmount: ..., couponCode: ... })`—instead of mutating the transaction.

findings.title: New response fields use snake_case; rest of the API uses camelCase
findings.message: The `Transaction` type (`src/domain/transaction.ts`) uses camelCase for all fields: `cardLast4`, `customerId`, `gatewayReference`, `createdAt`. The new fields `discount_amount` and `coupon_code` break this convention. Rename to `discountAmount` and `couponCode` to match the existing API contract.

findings.title: couponCode is read before schema validation and never validated
findings.message: `couponCode` is extracted from the raw body before `chargeSchema.parse()` and is never declared in `chargeSchema`. This means any arbitrary string (including very long ones) is accepted silently; unknown codes are just ignored with no feedback to the caller (`doStuff` returns the original amount). Add `couponCode: z.string().max(32).optional()` to `chargeSchema`, read it from the parsed `payload`, and consider returning a 400 for unrecognised codes rather than silently ignoring them.

findings.title: console.log used instead of the project's pino logger
findings.message: The project has a structured `pino` logger exported from `src/logger.ts` and used everywhere else (controllers, services, error handler). Replace `console.log('DEBUG: unknown coupon code:', couponCode)` with `logger.warn({ couponCode }, 'unknown_coupon_code')` to keep log output structured, level-controlled, and silenced during tests (the logger is configured `silent` in the test environment).

findings.title: Poor naming, var declarations, and loose equality throughout new file
findings.message: Several naming and style issues contradict TypeScript/project conventions: (1) `doStuff` is not a meaningful name—rename to `applyCoupon`. (2) Variables `tmp`, `result2`, `x` and constructor params `p`, `amt` are opaque—use `strategy`, `discountedAmount`, `discounted`, `percentage`, `fixedAmount`. (3) `var x` and `var tmp` should be `const`. (4) `code == 'WELCOME10'` and `tmp == null` should use strict `===`. None of these are blocking individually but collectively they make the code harder to maintain.

findings.title: No tests for coupon math or the controller integration
findings.message: The test plan explicitly marks unit tests for coupon math as a TODO. The new `FixedCouponStrategy` negative-amount edge case, the `PercentageCouponStrategy` floor rounding, and the controller's discount-amount calculation all need coverage. The existing `chargeRoute.test.ts` also does not assert that a `couponCode` request produces the correct `discountAmount`—meaning the broken `discount_amount: 0` bug would not be caught by `npm test`. Tests should be part of this PR, not deferred.

findings.title: Commented-out dead code should be removed
findings.message: The commented-out FREESHIP block (`// else if (code == 'FREESHIP')...`) should be removed. If it is needed in the future, it can be recovered from git history. Leaving it creates noise and false expectations.
